title: Bonney Sees Kuma’s Memories
description: On pages 2–4, this part focuses on Unknown person #1, Luffy, Unknown person #2, Unknown person #3, Kuma, Vegapunk as Bonney discovers Kuma’s memories, revealing his Buccaneer heritage, Ginny, the Revolutionary Army, God Valley, and the painful origins behind Kuma’s sacrifice.
scene: Bonney discovers Kuma’s memories, revealing his Buccaneer heritage, Ginny, the Revolutionary Army, God Valley, and the painful origins behind Kuma’s sacrifice.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: I'm afraid I'm here on business.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Man, it's cold! This place sure is a far cry from punk hazard.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Just you wait! The future will be born right here!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: My head is overflowing with ideas, it's honestly hard to think straight!!

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: At the future location of Egghead....?!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: That explains how you missed it...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: You really should've checked every nook and cranny of the lab when you moved in.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: navy Admiral "Kizaru" BORSALINOO

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: The government frowns on secret backroom deals with pirates, y'know.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I hate it when you get like this.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Okay, okay. You got me, borsalino.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: C'mon... I'm just doing my job...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: It's irrelevant if you consider him an ideal candidate, vegapunk...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: This man is a pirate with $2966 million on his head!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: You can say you were ignorant of that, or simply admit you don't care...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: ...but making a fugitive the face of our clone program is out of the question!! It would be an affront to the Navy's reputation!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: With that in mind, we have three additional stipulations if you want us to honor this deal of yours.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: Firstly, Kuma...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: You must agree to become one of the seven warlords of the sea!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: A few days ago, we found ourselves a member short thanks to the exploits of a rookie named Ace.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: It caused great turbulence in the pirate world. Someone of your caliber would be a natural choice to fill that opening and calm the seas.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: Our second condition...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: ...is for you to undergo procedures to become a human weapon for the navy!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: Are you talking about modifying my body...?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Precisely. You already have all the traits required to be an effective warlord, so you'll become a paragon of fear for pirate scum far and wide after you receive your enhancements.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I second that, the Navy would love to have you on our side.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Can it, Kizarui!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: You see my point... but that's not all. You aren't like other pirates, Kuma. We are well aware of your history with the revolutionary army.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: If you accept our offer, we must take precautions to ensure you never turn your power against the world government again.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: which brings us to our third and final condition...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: You must agree to give up...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: ...your free will and sense of indivi- duality.

summary: This part advances the Kuma and Bonney’s Tragedy through 32 cleaned dialogue beats, focusing on character decisions, battlefield momentum, and the story’s larger consequences.
